```{python}
#| echo: false
# Additional baseline conversion rates: 30% and 65%

def _peak_stats_cr(outcomes, avg_days_list, norm, cr_val, ptn_val, round_peak=False):
    y    = np.array(outcomes) / norm
    x    = 1 - p_thresholds
    peak = int(np.argmax(np.round(y * 100, 4)) if round_peak else np.argmax(y))
    opt_alpha       = x[peak]
    marginal_events = (y[peak] - cr_val) * (ptn_val + MAX_TEST_N)
    avg_d           = avg_days_list[peak]
    return f"""<div style="text-align:center; font-size:0.9rem; color:#333; margin-top:0.25rem;">
      α: <strong>{opt_alpha:.2f}</strong> &nbsp;|&nbsp;
      Marginal Events vs No Test: <strong>{marginal_events:,.0f}</strong> &nbsp;|&nbsp;
      Avg(Days): <strong>{avg_d:.1f}</strong>
    </div>"""

_cr_combos    = [(0.30, 'cr30'), (0.65, 'cr65')]
_sigma_combos = [(ITC_SE, 's1'), (2, 's2'), (0.5, 's05')]
_ptn_combos   = [(100_000, '100k'), (POST_TEST_N, '500k'), (POST_TEST_N * 4, '2m')]

for _cr_val, _cr_key in _cr_combos:
    for _itc_se_val, _sigma_key in _sigma_combos:
        _df_tmp = se.create_simulation_df(
            number_test_days=NUMBER_TEST_DAYS,
            number_simulations=NUMBER_SIMULATIONS,
            seed=SEED,
            condition_parameters=[{"mean": _cr_val, "trials": int(MAX_TEST_N * SPLIT)}],
        )
        _params_tmp = {"itc_mean": ITC_MEAN, "itc_se": _itc_se_val, "trials": int(MAX_TEST_N * SPLIT)}
        _df_tmp = se.add_random_effect_condition(_df_tmp, _params_tmp)
        _df_tmp = se.add_cumulative_columns(_df_tmp)
        _df_tmp = se.compute_cumulative_std_rates(_df_tmp)
        _df_tmp = se.compute_delta_stats(_df_tmp)
        _df_tmp = se.compute_test_stats(_df_tmp)
        _df_tmp = se.calculate_remaining_test_potential(_df_tmp)

        for _ptn_val, _ptn_key in _ptn_combos:
            _df_ptn = se.calculate_launch_potential(_df_tmp, post_test_n=_ptn_val)
            _df_ptn = se.calculate_total_potential(_df_ptn)
            _norm   = _df_ptn["total_trials_cumsum"].max() + _ptn_val

            _df_prepped_cr = prepare_stopping_decision_columns(
                _df_ptn.filter(pl.col("day") >= MIN_TEST_DAYS),
                metric_direction_improvement="increase",
            ).sort(["simulation", "day"])

            _df_fixed_cr = prepare_stopping_decision_columns(
                _df_ptn.filter(pl.col("day") >= NUMBER_TEST_DAYS),
                metric_direction_improvement="increase",
            ).sort(["simulation", "day"])

            _o1, _t1, _m1, _r1, _d1 = run_loop_df(_df_prepped_cr, True)
            _o2, _t2, _m2, _r2, _d2 = run_loop_df(_df_prepped_cr, False)
            _o3, _t3, _m3, _r3, _d3 = run_loop_df(_df_fixed_cr, False)

            _row_cr   = make_scenario_row(
                _o1, _t1, _m1, _r1,
                _o2, _t2, _m2, _r2,
                _o3, _t3, _m3, _r3,
                _norm, _cr_val, _ptn_val + MAX_TEST_N,
            )
            _stats_cr = make_scenario_stats(
                _o1, _d1, _t1, _r1, _o2, _d2, _t2, _r2, _o3, _d3, _t3, _r3,
                _norm, _cr_val, _ptn_val + MAX_TEST_N,
            )

            _key = f"{_ptn_key}_{_sigma_key}_{_cr_key}"
            display(HTML(f"""<script>
window.__wif_scenarios = window.__wif_scenarios || {{}};
window.__wif_scenarios['{_key}'] = {{
  row1:  {pio.to_json(_row_cr)},
  stats: {json.dumps(_stats_cr)}
}};
</script>"""))
```

::: {style="margin-top:0.25rem;"}
# Insights
:::

```{=html}
<div style="font-size:1rem; color:#666; margin-top:-0.75rem; margin-bottom:0.75rem;">&#9432;&nbsp; Click a finding to highlight the relevant rows in the results table above.</div>

<div id="insights-list" style="margin-top:0.5rem; display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.6rem;">

  <label style="display:flex; align-items:flex-start; gap:0.65rem; cursor:pointer; font-size:0.95rem; line-height:1.5;">
    <input type="radio" name="insight" value="peeking-best" checked
           style="margin-top:3px; accent-color:#1e46a0; cursor:pointer; flex-shrink:0;">
    <span><strong>Peeking + Best is the most profitable strategy.</strong> Stopping early when you have a signal and deploying the leader when you don't beats every alternative — it captures upside faster and never wastes an inconclusive experiment by defaulting to control.</span>
  </label>

  <label style="display:flex; align-items:flex-start; gap:0.65rem; cursor:pointer; font-size:0.95rem; line-height:1.5;">
    <input type="radio" name="insight" value="phacking"
           style="margin-top:3px; accent-color:#1e46a0; cursor:pointer; flex-shrink:0;">
    <span><strong>P-Hacking is Good For Business:</strong> Flouting the rules of Fixed Sample <em>Significance Testing</em> — like peeking and retaining Best Performer when result is inconclusive — is good for business.</span>
  </label>

  <label style="display:flex; align-items:flex-start; gap:0.65rem; cursor:pointer; font-size:0.95rem; line-height:1.5;">
    <input type="radio" name="insight" value="alpha50"
           style="margin-top:3px; accent-color:#1e46a0; cursor:pointer; flex-shrink:0;">
    <span><strong>The optimal α for a fixed sample test is 0.50</strong> — deploy whoever is leading on the final day, every time. Conventional thresholds don't just leave value on the table; they actively cause you to default to control when the data already points elsewhere.</span>
  </label>

  <label style="display:flex; align-items:flex-start; gap:0.65rem; cursor:pointer; font-size:0.95rem; line-height:1.5;">
    <input type="radio" name="insight" value="peeking-vs-fixed"
           style="margin-top:3px; accent-color:#1e46a0; cursor:pointer; flex-shrink:0;">
    <span><strong>Peeking vs Fixed Sample (α = 0.50) is a toss-up.</strong> Both strategies produce similar outcomes — but Peeking gets there in roughly half the trials, at the cost of lower accuracy in picking the true winner.</span>
  </label>

</div>

<script>
(function() {
  var HIGHLIGHTS = {
    'peeking-best': function(row) {
      return row.getAttribute('data-label') === 'Peeking + Best';
    },
    'phacking': function(row) {
      var lbl    = row.getAttribute('data-label')  || '';
      var is_ref = row.getAttribute('data-is-ref') === 'true';
      return lbl === 'Peeking + Best' || lbl === 'Peeking' || (lbl === 'Fixed Sample' && is_ref);
    },
    'alpha50': function(row) {
      return row.getAttribute('data-label') === 'Fixed Sample';
    },
    'peeking-vs-fixed': function(row) {
      var lbl    = row.getAttribute('data-label')  || '';
      var is_ref = row.getAttribute('data-is-ref') === 'true';
      return lbl === 'Peeking' || (lbl === 'Fixed Sample' && !is_ref);
    }
  };

  function applyHighlights() {
    var active = document.querySelector('input[name="insight"]:checked');
    var tbody  = document.querySelector('#wif-stats1 tbody');
    if (!tbody) return;
    tbody.querySelectorAll('tr').forEach(function(row) {
      var hit = active && HIGHLIGHTS[active.value] && HIGHLIGHTS[active.value](row);
      row.style.background   = hit ? '#fffbea' : '';
      row.style.outline      = hit ? '1.5px solid #f0a500' : '';
      row.style.outlineOffset = hit ? '-1px' : '';
    });
  }

  // Re-apply whenever the table is rebuilt
  function watchStats() {
    var el = document.getElementById('wif-stats1');
    if (!el) { setTimeout(watchStats, 200); return; }
    new MutationObserver(applyHighlights).observe(el, { childList: true, subtree: true });
    applyHighlights();
  }
  watchStats();

  document.addEventListener('change', function(e) {
    if (e.target && e.target.name === 'insight') {
      applyHighlights();
      var table = document.getElementById('wif-stats1');
      if (table) {
        var top = table.getBoundingClientRect().top + window.pageYOffset - 80;
        window.scrollTo({ top: top, behavior: 'smooth' });
      }
    }
  });
})();
</script>
```


```{=html}
<p style="margin-top:1.5rem;"><strong>Long story short:</strong> Significance testing protects you from being wrong. It doesn't optimize for being right. That caution has a measurable cost — value lost to experiments that run longer than needed, and value surrendered every time you default to control when the data already points elsewhere.</p>
```

# Code Snippets

Two reusable pieces from the analysis — simulating the control arm and simulating the treatment arm with a randomly drawn true effect. Both generate all 100,000 simulations in a single NumPy call — no for loops.

```{=html}
<div class="mt-3 mb-2">
  <div class="accordion-item" style="border: 1.5px dashed #90b8e0; border-radius: 6px; background: #f0f6fd; margin-bottom: 0.5rem;">
    <div class="accordion-header" style="margin:0;">
      <button class="accordion-button collapsed px-3 shadow-none"
              style="font-size:0.9rem; font-weight:600; color:#1e46a0; background:transparent; height:auto; width:100%; display:flex; align-items:center; padding-top:0.5rem; padding-bottom:0.5rem; flex-wrap:wrap;"
              type="button" data-bs-toggle="collapse" data-bs-target="#code-control-collapse">
        <span style="flex:1;">Simulating the Control</span>
        <span class="accordion-chevron" style="flex-shrink:0; margin-left:0.75rem; font-size:1.8rem; line-height:1; transition:transform 0.2s;">&#8964;</span>
        <span style="width:100%; font-size:0.85rem; font-weight:400; font-style:italic; color:#555; margin-top:0.2rem;">Each simulation of control is an independent binomial process — same true rate, but different random outcome.</span>
      </button>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div id="code-control-collapse" class="collapse" style="border: 1.5px dashed #90b8e0; border-top: none; border-radius: 0 0 6px 6px; padding: 1rem; background: #fff; margin-bottom: 0.5rem;">
    <p style="font-size:0.9rem; margin-bottom:0.75rem;"><code>create_simulation_df</code> generates a DataFrame of daily binomial draws for one or more fixed-rate conditions. Each row is one day of one simulation.</p>
    <pre style="background:#f8f8f8; border-radius:4px; padding:1rem; font-size:0.8rem; overflow-x:auto;"><code>import numpy as np
import polars as pl


def create_simulation_df(
    number_test_days,
    number_simulations,
    condition_parameters,
    seed=None,
):
    if seed is not None:
        np.random.seed(seed)

    # trials_per_day is the same for every day in the experiment
    n = np.array(
        [c["trials"] // number_test_days for c in condition_parameters]
    )[:, None]
    p = np.array([c["mean"] for c in condition_parameters])[:, None]

    rep = number_test_days * number_simulations
    n_mat = np.repeat(n, rep, axis=1)
    p_mat = np.repeat(p, rep, axis=1)

    # One binomial draw per (condition, day, simulation)
    samples = np.random.binomial(n=n_mat, p=p_mat)

    return pl.DataFrame(
        {
            "simulation": np.arange(rep) // number_test_days + 1,
            "day": np.arange(rep) % number_test_days + 1,
            **{
                f"condition_{i}_value": samples[i]
                for i in range(len(condition_parameters))
            },
            **{
                f"condition_{i}_trials": n_mat[i]
                for i in range(len(condition_parameters))
            },
            **{
                f"condition_{i}_true_mean": np.full(
                    rep, condition_parameters[i]["mean"]
                )
                for i in range(len(condition_parameters))
            },
        }
    )</code></pre>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="mt-3 mb-2">
  <div class="accordion-item" style="border: 1.5px dashed #90b8e0; border-radius: 6px; background: #f0f6fd; margin-bottom: 0.5rem;">
    <div class="accordion-header" style="margin:0;">
      <button class="accordion-button collapsed px-3 shadow-none"
              style="font-size:0.9rem; font-weight:600; color:#1e46a0; background:transparent; height:auto; width:100%; display:flex; align-items:center; padding-top:0.5rem; padding-bottom:0.5rem; flex-wrap:wrap;"
              type="button" data-bs-toggle="collapse" data-bs-target="#code-treatment-collapse">
        <span style="flex:1;">Simulating the Treatment</span>
        <span class="accordion-chevron" style="flex-shrink:0; margin-left:0.75rem; font-size:1.8rem; line-height:1; transition:transform 0.2s;">&#8964;</span>
        <span style="width:100%; font-size:0.85rem; font-weight:400; font-style:italic; color:#555; margin-top:0.2rem;">Each simulation of treatment is encoded with a different true effect — sometimes harmful, sometimes beneficial, often marginal.</span>
      </button>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div id="code-treatment-collapse" class="collapse" style="border: 1.5px dashed #90b8e0; border-top: none; border-radius: 0 0 6px 6px; padding: 1rem; background: #fff; margin-bottom: 0.5rem;">
    <p style="font-size:0.9rem; margin-bottom:0.75rem;"><code>add_random_effect_condition</code> appends a treatment arm where the true effect varies across simulations but is fixed within one. The effect is drawn from an ITC (Index-to-Control) distribution: ITC = 100 &#8594; no lift; ITC ~ N(100, 1) &#8594; effects centered at parity with modest spread.</p>
    <pre style="background:#f8f8f8; border-radius:4px; padding:1rem; font-size:0.8rem; overflow-x:auto;"><code>import numpy as np
import polars as pl


def add_random_effect_condition(df, random_effect_parameters, seed=None):
    if seed is not None:
        np.random.seed(seed)

    n_simulations = int(df["simulation"].max())
    n_days = int(df["day"].max())

    # One ITC draw per simulation — the unknown true effect for that experiment
    itc_samples = (
        np.random.normal(
            random_effect_parameters["itc_mean"],
            random_effect_parameters["itc_se"],
            n_simulations,
        )
        / 100
    )

    # treatment_mean = (itc / 100) x control_mean
    control_mean = float(df["condition_0_true_mean"][0])
    treatment_means = itc_samples * control_mean

    # Daily binomial draws; treatment_mean is constant across days within a simulation
    trials_per_day = random_effect_parameters["trials"] // n_days
    events = np.random.binomial(
        n=trials_per_day,
        p=treatment_means[:, None],
        size=(n_simulations, n_days),
    )

    # Reshape to tall format to match the existing DataFrame
    next_condition = (
        max(
            int(c.split("_")[1])
            for c in df.columns
            if c.startswith("condition_") and c.split("_")[1].isdigit()
        )
        + 1
    )
    return df.with_columns(
        [
            pl.Series(
                f"condition_{next_condition}_value",
                events.flatten(),
            ),
            pl.Series(
                f"condition_{next_condition}_trials",
                np.full(n_simulations * n_days, trials_per_day),
            ),
            pl.Series(
                f"condition_{next_condition}_true_mean",
                np.repeat(treatment_means, n_days),
            ),
        ]
    )</code></pre>
  </div>
</div>
<p style="margin-top:1rem; font-size:0.95rem; color:#555;">Want to see more? <a href="mailto:rzaliznyak@gmail.com">Let me know</a>.</p>
```
